mnemonics: TRY to CATCH errors gracefully: Imagine trying to catch a ball (code execution). If you miss (exception), you have a backup plan (catch block) to prevent it from crashing.
keyPoints: Use try to enclose code that might throw an exception.
Use catch to handle specific exceptions and provide recovery code.
A try block must be followed by at least one catch or a finally block.
commonMistakes: Using overly broad catch blocks (like catch(Exception e)) which can hide specific errors.
Not providing any meaningful recovery or logging in the catch block, making debugging difficult.
Placing too much code in the try block, making it hard to identify the exact source of an exception.
